title: Morty and the Monster Truck Madness

q.en: What event did Morty, Ben, and Fred want to attend?
q.zh: 莫蒂、本和弗雷德想去参加什么活动？

options.en: The Monster Truck Madness rally in Mousetown
options.zh: 鼠镇举行的怪兽卡车狂热表演赛

explain.en: The Mousetown Gazette advertised 'Monster Truck Madness: Coming to Mousetown in Two Weeks!' and the mice cried, 'We have to go!'
explain.zh: 《鼠镇公报》刊登了“怪兽卡车狂热：两周后来到鼠镇！”的广告，老鼠们大喊：“我们一定得去！”

q.en: How much did the discounted tickets on Fred's flyer cost?
q.zh: 弗雷德传单上的打折票每张多少钱？

explain.en: Fred's flyer read 'FOUR MONSTER TRUCK TICKETS ... $40 each,' and Ben noted they were 'ten dollars cheaper than the original price.'
explain.zh: 弗雷德的传单上写着“四张怪兽卡车票……每张40美元”，本注意到这比原价“便宜了10美元”。

q.en: In the book, what is a 'rally'?
q.zh: 在书中，“rally（集会/表演赛）”是什么？

options.en: A race or show where vehicles are driven over roads or rough terrain
options.zh: 一种让车辆在道路上或崎岖地形上行驶的比赛或表演

explain.en: The glossary defines 'rally' as 'a race or show where vehicles are driven over roads or rough terrain.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将“rally”定义为“一种让车辆在道路上或崎岖地形上行驶的比赛或表演”。

q.en: How did Morty get the $20 he needed for his ticket?
q.zh: 莫蒂是怎样弄到买票所需的20美元的？

options.en: He took it from his mother's wallet without asking
options.zh: 他没经允许从母亲的钱包里拿的

options.en: He earned it by selling lemonade
His father gave him a loan
He found it on the ground

explain.en: Morty saw four five-dollar bills in his mother's wallet and 'plucked the money from her wallet and pocketed it' to pay for the ticket.
explain.zh: 莫蒂看到母亲钱包里有4张5美元钞票，便“从钱包里抽出钱塞进口袋”去付票款。

q.en: Why did Morty's mother have to put most of her groceries back at the store?
q.zh: 为什么莫蒂的母亲在商店只好把大部分食品放回去？

options.en: Because the rally was that day
Because the $20 Morty took left her twenty dollars short at checkout
options.zh: 因为莫蒂拿走的20美元让她结账时少了20美元

explain.en: Mother said, 'I was twenty dollars short, so I had to put most of the groceries back,' which happened because Morty had taken the money.
explain.zh: 母亲说：“我少了20美元，所以只好把大部分食品放回去。”这正是莫蒂拿走钱所导致的。

q.en: What important lesson does Mother teach Morty?
q.zh: 母亲教给莫蒂什么重要的道理？

options.en: Monster trucks are more important than family
options.zh: 怪兽卡车比家人更重要

options.en: His honest reputation is worth more than all the money there is
options.zh: 他诚实的名誉比所有的钱都重要

options.en: Stealing is a good way to get what you want
options.zh: 偷窃是得到想要东西的好办法

explain.en: Mother tells Morty, 'Stealing is a crime' and 'Your honest reputation is worth more than all the money there is.'
explain.zh: 母亲告诉莫蒂：“偷窃是犯罪”，而且“你诚实的名誉比所有的钱都重要”。

q.en: What did Morty do to make up for his mistake?
q.zh: 莫蒂为了弥补自己的错误做了什么？

options.en: He sold his ticket, paid his mother back, and promised never to take anything without permission
options.zh: 他卖掉了票、把钱还给了母亲，并承诺未经允许绝不再拿任何东西

options.en: He went to the rally anyway

explain.en: Morty sold his ticket to Gus, handed his mother a twenty-dollar bill, and said, 'You can trust me to never take anything from you without your permission.'
explain.zh: 莫蒂把票卖给了格斯，把20美元还给母亲，并说：“你可以相信我，未经你的允许我绝不再拿你的任何东西。”

q.en: When Fred says 'You're bailing?' what does 'bailing' mean here?
q.zh: 当弗雷德说“你要打退堂鼓（bailing）？”时，这里的“bailing”是什么意思？

options.en: Escaping from an unwanted situation or abandoning a responsibility
options.zh: 从不想面对的处境中逃脱，或放弃责任

explain.en: The glossary defines 'bailing' as 'escaping from an unwanted situation or abandoning a responsibility.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将“bailing”定义为“从不想面对的处境中逃脱，或放弃责任”。

q.en: Why does the author show Morty returning the money and giving his word?
q.zh: 作者为什么要描写莫蒂还钱并作出承诺？

options.zh: 为了讲解贷款如何运作

options.en: To prove monster trucks are fun
options.zh: 为了证明怪兽卡车很好玩

options.en: To show that Morty learned the value of trust and honesty
options.zh: 为了表现莫蒂懂得了信任与诚实的价值

explain.en: The story ends with Morty earning back his mother's trust, showing the author's message that honesty matters more than the rally.
explain.zh: 故事以莫蒂重新赢得母亲的信任结尾，表明作者想要传达“诚实比表演赛更重要”的信息。

q.en: The book asks: if a $20.00 loan had an extra 25 cents charged each day, how much would be owed after one week?
q.zh: 书中有道题：如果20美元的贷款每天额外收取25美分，一周后一共要还多少钱？

explain.en: $20.00 plus 7 days times $0.25 equals $20.00 + $1.75 = $21.75, as the book's math question sets up.
explain.zh: 20美元加上7天乘以0.25美元，等于20美元加1.75美元，即21.75美元，正如书中数学题所设。

prompt.en: Have you ever wanted something so much that you made a poor choice? What would you do differently after reading Morty's story?
prompt.zh: 你有没有因为太想要某样东西而做出糟糕选择的时候？读了莫蒂的故事后，你会怎么做才不同？

en: Think about how Morty felt when his mother could not buy groceries.
Consider why earning trust back is harder than earning money.
zh: 想想莫蒂的母亲买不起食品时，莫蒂心里的感受。
想一想为什么重新赢得信任比挣钱更难。
